meta.title: Yunodai Ski Resort
meta.description: A retro alpine and cross-country resort with deep snow and access to historic Hijiori Onsen, open until May.

hero.title:
Yunodai
Ski Resort
hero.tagline: A retro alpine and cross-country resort with deep snow and access to historic Hijiori Onsen, open until May.

lift1.label: Anvár lift
lift1.note: Rare sliding surface lift in Japan

sanctuary.title:
Showa heritage—
master the Anvár.
sanctuary.lead: No chairlift, no gondola—just one dwindling Anvár surface lift on a compact 5 ha hill. Reviews praise snow quality and slope; the antidote to commoditized mega-resorts for core skiers who want spring laps in peace.
sanctuary.noticeBody: "Parking to the lift is too far"—formalizing paid snowmobile shuttles turns the pain point into revenue and better UX. Don't hide the friction; reframe it as value.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Spring corn snow—a quiet run at Yunodai Ski Resort

primary.title: Until early May × deep snow
primary.lead: Yamagata's earliest open and latest close—spring skiing is the headline weapon. Don't fight Mt. Gassan on scale; win with Hijiori Onsen packaging.
primary.imgAlt: Late-season Yunodai—spring skiing under blue skies

secondary.title: SAJ cross-country course
secondary.lead: Independent value from alpine—a full 3 km XC loop hosting national events. Foundation for Nordic & Toji wellness tourism.

pass.title:
Hijiori Onsen—
snow and hot spring in one day.
pass.lead: Bundle a Yunodai day lift ticket, lunch at partner eateries (torimotsu ramen, etc.), and a day-use onsen voucher—moving beyond a two-hour ski stop into a 1,200-year toji economy.
pass.imgAlt: Hijiori fantasy snow corridor—historic onsen street lit by snow walls

hike.title:
Snowmobile
Express.
hike.lead: The walk from parking to the lift is the top complaint—reviews celebrate informal snowmobile rides. A paid official option (e.g. ¥500 round trip) fixes UX and adds revenue.
hike.leadCurry: After skiing, head to Hijiori—Inakaya's torimotsu ramen or Kotobuki soba to warm up before the baths.
hike.imgAlt: Hijiori torimotsu ramen—local snow-country comfort food

tour.title:
Deep snow and secret onsen—
one-day detox.
tour.lead: For core skiers and wellness travelers—a full-day snow-and-soak loop, not a ski-only visit.

04.title: Hijiori Onsen town
04.body: Snow corridor stroll · Kaneyama liquor shop

05.title: Ryokan stay
05.body: Optional Hijiori morning market next day

sanctuary.title: Complete spring ski guide
sanctuary.body: Open from early December through early May—don't compete with Kamimuro (March close) or Gassan on scale. Own the niche: longest season plus Hijiori Onsen.

workation.title: Anvár Survivor certification
workation.body: "Conquer Yamagata's last secret lift"—gamify the tricky Anvár. Share on SNS for a survivor sticker and tap core skiers' bragging rights.

lake.title: Nordic & Toji wellness
lake.body: Pair SAJ XC aerobic training with 1,200-year Hijiori toji—modern health tourism. The Nordic hall has no Wi-Fi or showers; use onsen lodgings as base camp.

access.note: Village bus from JR Shinjo ~50 min, alight Makiba-mae + 2 min walk · ~30 min from Funagata IC · 50 free parking spaces
